publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-05 18:18:11 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Price, David, 1940-1999. (Title of work: Traditional and transformational analysis of Sherente kinship terminology..)

did.physdesc: Audio Recordings, Correspondence, Diaries, Manuscripts, Photographs, Research Materials
did.abstract: Published and unpublished stories and articles by Edgar Price and genealogical material concerning the Price family. Correspondence from between family members. Typescript field notes by David Price on Nambiquara (Satones) Indian groups in Brazil. Notes, tape recordings, memos, published and unpublished reports used to prepare book about Nambicuara Indians of Brazil; and autobiographical writing by David Price.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, diaries, travel albums and journals, accounts, financial and legal papers, post cards and photographs, published and unpublished stories and articles by Edgar Price, advertising copy and other writing, and genealogical material concerning the Price family, primarily Edgar Dayton Price, Paul D. Price, Arthur B. Price, Francis Smith Price (Mrs. Edgar D.), and Florence Price Beeson, and including letters and a silk color sample book from her dressmaking establishment in Shanghai, China.

Also, correspondence from Paul and Hazel Price to David Price, 1943-1982; correspondence, notes, notebooks, population data, and other material written by David Price in the 1950s and typescript field notes by David Price on Nambiquara (Satones) (Nambicuara) Indian groups in Brazil, 1967-1970. Notes, tape recordings, memos, and published and unpublished reports used to prepare his book about Nambiquara; and autobiographical writing by David Price,1998.
Also, Master's thesis that David Price wrote at the University of Chicago, "A Traditional and Transformational Analysis of Sherente Kinship Terminology," 1967, Bd. Ms. 1.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Price family papers, #4082. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to the finding aid. PDF file.

Letters from Paul to Ma and Pa

Aunt Florence's letters from the Orient

The House Beautiful; The Gasoline Housemaid
Lady Cow and Her Bed Chamber
Leading Men in the Hardware Trade; The Suburban Hardware Store
Marketing Motorcycles thru the Hardware Dealer
Our Business Bugbear; Pushing Lantern Sales
Rope and Cordage in the Hardware Store
The Whirr of the Lawn Mower

The Boss and the Export Order
The Marketing of "A New Brand" of Cigars
The Pit He Digged for Another
Greater Newark - Letter to the Newark Sunday Call
The Marketing of the Sugar Loaf
Old Third Ward Recalls Halcyon Days for One Newarker
The First Attic Bank - German National Bank News
The Get-Rich-Quicks: The Saving Citizen; The Kosse State Bank News; Farmers & Merchants Bank Notes
R.E. Dietz Advertisement in Hardware Age (windows dressed by Paul D. Price)
Adds, Cards, Mementos of Ma's (Frances Smith Price)

Life With a Japanese Family by Paul D. Price

My Trip to Japan by Paul D. Price
Poems - originals - duplicates not sent to publishers
Poetry - duplicates of poems being sent to publishers
Story and Poems - Arthur Price to Florence Beesom
Robin Runaway - The Adventures of a Fairy
Part II Contemplations, Fancy & Imagination - In the Fairy Mood
Cruise to Istanbul and Edge of Black Sea; A Week in Italy; A Week in Egypt
Journal of the 1940 Kungsholm Cruise to the South Seas, by Arthur Price
Notes by the Way, Maine to Florida, Down the Mexican West Coast, Life in Florida, Quebec Tour, Travelling West
Cruise of the Saturnia to the Mediterranean Sea, Morocco, Portugal and the Islands of the Atlantic

Letters from Barbara Moons, Kit, Shirley, Cindy, Toni, Penny, Merry

Album and pages, Lake George, Lake Champlain and Saratoga

My Trip with Harry Edel to Canada
Typescript anthropology field notes taken by David Price in Brazil
Kin Term Definitions - Joã de Maxixe
Report: "The Integrated Development of Brazil's Northwest Frontier"
PNO - World Bank material - "may not be cited"
PNO - World Bank material - "may not be cited"
Autobiographical Notes ("Notes toward a biography about David Price")
Translation of ethnohistorical account of the people whose pot-sherds are found near Serra Azul
Translation of myth recounting origin of flutes and agriculture
Unsatisfactory attempt to elicit story of first coming of Brazilians
Translation of the myth of the Great Darkness
120: Address given when Macaquinho married Marta
170: How the Moon Got His Spots
Letter to Cecil Cook, an anthropologist who had returned to the US
Ethnohistory of people whose potsherds are found near Serra Azul
The Coming of the First Brazilians
Two songs (part of one has been inadvertently erased)
While in Cuiaba, Ourivaldo wrote out the words, then sang these songs
History of fights between the Sawentesu, Halotesu, Kithaulhu, and Wakalitesu
119 Curing: he was singing for his son in the house next door
Gives my relations, assuming we are x cousins
On the normative aspect of kinship
52 Speech by D.W. Snyder to FUNAI, defending South America mission
Attempt to get Baiano to talk about religion; small talk (hidden microphone)
25 Joking between Jose and Helena ["H" and "W"] on his departure
Attempt to get kids involved in joking behavior, with hidden mike
Songs whose lyrics he wrote down
News from Serra Azul to Camarare
Rendering of Vitorino's wedding address into understandable Nambiquara
Termite song, jaguar song, spirit-wife song, spirit speech
Evening singing concluding Miriam's puberty festival. Said to be typical of the leader's group.
Wolfgang Müller correspondence; Confirmation certificate from Trinity Church Irvington: New Jersey, by the Bishop of New Jersey Edwin Lines
Dissertation, "The Origins and Establishment of Brazil's Indian Service: 1889-1910" by David Hall Stauffer, University of Texas
Dissertation, "The Origins and Establishment of Brazil's Indian Service: 1889-1910" by David Hall Stauffer, University of Texas
Thesis, "Embodied Names: Construing Nambiquara Personhood Through Naming Practices" by Marcelo Oppido Fiorini, New York University
Photographs of native people and camp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of native people, village, huts, camp, including some aerial photos showing deforestation of the Guaporé Valley, Mato Grosso. Zillo Ranch by Pedro Agostinho, Federal University of Bahia.

did.unittitle: Thesis, "History and Survival of the Xavante Indians of Central Brazil" by Nancy M. Flowers, Hunter College
